page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AMD EPYC 9T34 processor clocked at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores align with the middle 50% average. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specific workloads, achieving top 25% placement in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Memory performance is also robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while cached read-only memory bandwidth averages 2465.48 GB/sec.

The instance offers a balanced resource profile, though the lack of bundled local storage requires external block storage attachment. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC cores deliver high single-core efficiency and strong cryptographic performance without the premium of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. This configuration is appropriate for enterprise applications, web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.8xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by a 3.4 GHz AMD EPYC 9T34 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU execution alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and cryptographic capabilities. The instance delivers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-effective option for general-purpose enterprise workloads. It is appropriate for web serving, database operations, and applications requiring robust single-core processing.
